title: skopeo logout doesn't remove docker config
body:
I just ran into the issue but did not verify it in the code:

```
$ podman pull/skopeo copy docker://quay.io/...
-> wrong credentials
$ podman/skopeo logout quay.io
-> not logged in
```

The debug log revealed we've been reading from `/root/.docker/config.json`.  If my theory is correct, that logout does not look at this config, I think it should.

comments.body:
Those are not exact error messages, are they? That’s making it a bit difficult to figure out what’s going on.

Login/logout only _operates_ on helpers and `auth.json`. Logout does actually have code to detect that Docker config contains credentials; that seems to be reporting incorrect text in the (credentials present, `authInvalid`) case.

If that’s the cause, the code is in c/common/pkg/auth.

I must have missed the error message. Reproducing yields the following quite useful error message:

> Not logged into quay.io with current tool. Existing credentials were established via docker login. Please use docker logout instead.
